step_by_step_thinking:
True statements regarding the calculation of HDI are all of the following except:
A: In gross enrolment, only secondary education is considered, not primary education.
B: 1/3rd weightage is given to adult literacy.
C: 2/3rds weightage is given to gross enrollment.
D: Country's achievement is computed on the basis of adult literacy.
From reviewing the provided articles, none contain explicit details about calculating the Human Development Index (HDI). Therefore we'll rely purely on external knowledge here. Typically,
- Gross Enrolment Ratio considers tertiary & secondary education together, thus A could potentially be false since primary isn't excluded explicitly either way via typical definitions.
- HDI indeed gives approximately 1/3 weight to adult literacy according to its formula.
- Around two-thirds go towards expected years of schooling/Gross Enrolment ratio per UNDP guidelines (which might slightly vary year-to-year).
- While HDI uses indicators related to human capital/literacy/etc., it doesn’t entirely compute country achievements solely off adult literacy - other dimensions factor heavily too e.g. life expectancy and income indices.
So considering common HDI framework elements,
\boxed{C} appears incorrect among listed options relative to typical HDI construction involving roughly equal splits across different parameters rather than just focusing excessively on gross enrollment ratios ignoring others. Thus making C stand out incorrectly stated within these constraints. ```plaintext
raw_response:
True statements regarding the calculation of HDI are all of the following except:
A: In gross enrolment, only secondary education is considered, not primary education.
B: 1/3rd weightage is given to adult literacy.
C: 2/3rds weightage is given to gross enrollment.
D: Country's achievement is computed on the basis of adult literacy.
From reviewing the provided articles, none contain explicit details about calculating the Human Development Index (HDI). Therefore we'll rely purely on external knowledge here. Typically,
- Gross Enrolment Ratio considers tertiary & secondary education together, thus A could potentially be false since primary isn't excluded explicitly either way via typical definitions.
- HDI indeed gives approximately 1/3 weight to adult literacy according to its formula.
- Around two-thirds go towards expected years of schooling/Gross Enrolment ratio per UNDP guidelines (which might slightly vary year-to-year).
- While HDI uses indicators related to human capital/literacy/etc., it doesn’t entirely compute country achievements solely off adult literacy - other dimensions factor heavily too e.g. life expectancy and income indices.
So considering common HDI framework elements,
\boxed{C} appears incorrect among listed options relative to typical HDI construction involving roughly equal splits across different parameters rather than just focusing excessively on gross enrollment ratios ignoring others. Thus making C stand out incorrectly stated within these constraints. ```plaintext
